text.title: License Allowlist with Hard Block on Copyleft for Proprietary Builds
text.summary: Pin a per-product license allowlist, block copyleft contamination, and force the agent to escalate any non-allowlist license to a human.

section.title: Allow / flag / deny per dependency
section.p: License risk depends on distribution. SaaS-only products tolerate Apache/MIT/BSD/MPL but cannot tolerate AGPL. On-prem distributed binaries also forbid GPL except where the build can comply. The policy is encoded as data, not as judgement, so an agent can apply it without ambiguity.

rule.statement: Every repo MUST commit a `license-policy.yml` (or equivalent FOSSA / Black Duck policy) listing `allow`, `flag`, and `deny` SPDX identifiers per build target. The CI MUST fail when any dependency resolves to a `deny` license OR introduces a license not in `allow` / `flag`. For proprietary SaaS the deny list MUST include at minimum `GPL-2.0-only`, `GPL-3.0-only`, `AGPL-3.0-only`, `SSPL-1.0`. The agent MUST refuse to add a dependency whose declared license is not on the allowlist; if uncertain, escalate to a human, never silently accept. On every dependency change the agent MUST regenerate `NOTICE` / `THIRD_PARTY.md` from the lockfile, not by hand.
rule.rationale: Encoding the policy as machine-readable data turns license review from a once-a-quarter human task into a per-PR build gate; the agent gets a deterministic boundary instead of having to "decide" what is acceptable.

example.code: # license-policy.yml target: saas allow: [MIT, Apache-2.0, BSD-2-Clause, BSD-3-Clause, ISC, MPL-2.0] flag: [LGPL-2.1-only, LGPL-3.0-only, EPL-2.0] # need legal review deny: [GPL-2.0-only, GPL-3.0-only, AGPL-3.0-only, SSPL-1.0, BUSL-1.1] unknown: deny
example.note: `unknown: deny` forces the agent to escalate when license metadata is missing.

example.code: # Effective policy: "anything goes if tests pass" license-check: false
example.note: No policy means the agent accepts AGPL into a SaaS, triggering source-disclosure obligation across the stack.

antipattern.description: Adding a dep with an unknown license and writing a comment "license to be reviewed later" so the build can move.
antipattern.reason: The "later" review never happens; the obligation propagates with every release until a customer audit surfaces it.

section.reference: CI workflow running licensee + SBOM on every PR.
